abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
               <jats:p>The paper aims to give a unified account of the central concepts in recent work on bandit processes and dynamic allocation indices; to show how these reduce some previously intractable problems to the problem of calculating such indices; and to describe how these calculations may be carried out. Applications to stochastic scheduling, sequential clinical trials and a class of search problems are discussed.</jats:p>
title: Bandit Processes and Dynamic Allocation Indices
container-title: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology

title: A Dynamic Allocation Index for the Discounted Multiarmed Bandit Problem
A dynamic allocation index for the discounted multiarmed bandit problem

abstract: <jats:p>Stoppable families of alternative bandit processes are decision processes with the property that at each decision epoch the choice is between allocating service to one of the constituent bandit processes or stopping and deciding in favour of one of them. The problem is considered of finding optimal (or good suboptimal) strategies for such processes. The theory for non-stoppable families leads us to study the performance of a simple strategy. This is shown to be optimal under certain conditions. These conditions are discussed and an example relating to research planning is given.</jats:p>
title: Stoppable families of alternative bandit processes
